results.possibleIdentity: John E. Kaufman (1927–1998) was a prominent American lighting engineer and technical leader in the field of illumination. He served for many years as the Technical Director of the Illuminating Engineering Society (IES) of North America (now IESNA), based in New York City. He is most widely recognized as the long-time editor of the 'IES Lighting Handbook,' often referred to as the definitive reference in the lighting industry, having edited editions in 1966, 1972, 1981, and 1984. His expertise in 'illuminating engineering' spanned a wide range of applications, including industrial, commercial, and museum lighting, and he was involved with the IES Committee on Museum and Art Gallery Lighting. Kaufman graduated from Cornell University in 1949 and was a member of the Cornell Society of Engineers. Throughout his career, he held leadership roles in the Commission Internationale de l'Eclairage (CIE) and was an active member of the New York Academy of Science. Given his role as a technical authority in New York during the late 1960s and early 1970s, he was associated with the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) technical services registry, which connected artists with technical professionals for collaborative projects.
